decisions.decision: Create a new playbook from scratch; exclude the supplied all-in-one source document.
Use the source only to derive independently authored process behavior and templates.
Alpha supports guided/manual/dry-run artifact generation and does not mutate external systems.
Use playbook ID external.workflow_preparation and version 0.1.0-alpha.1.
Unresolved non-blocking policy choices are recorded after alpha rather than interrupting creation.
Production creates copy-ready documents only; the analyst manually publishes them to external documentation system and work-tracking system.
Production execution is manual by an analyst; no sanctioned automatic runtime adapter is required for the first production scope.
Cancelled runs are terminal and non-resumable.
Use a two-stage package lifecycle: draft review first, then external publication and URL synchronization, then a linked package revision.
external documentation system URL is captured before work-tracking system creation so the work-tracking system draft can include the passport link.
External-link insertion increments the run package revision, not the playbook specification SemVer.
Only the Workflow Preparation work-tracking system URL is mandatory for final readiness; implementation work-tracking system URL is optional downstream metadata.
The analyst alone reviews the downloaded draft and must explicitly confirm it before external publication steps begin.
No second human approval is required after URL synchronization; deterministic validation is sufficient when business content is unchanged.
The playbook does not enforce work-tracking system or external documentation system host/domain allowlists and does not test remote accessibility.
The final package stores only the external process record URL and work-tracking record URL as lifecycle links.
Temporary runtime evidence and personal sign-off working data are retained only until process completion; the final package remains the durable output.
The model may propose a minimum approver set by data mode, but Product/PO must confirm or edit it; the model is never an approver and cannot record approval for a human.
